quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 5 Midterm Review
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Circular Motion
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A freight locomotive traveling at $34.0\text{ m/s}$ applies its brakes, causing a uniform deceleration of $1.25\text{ m/s}^2$. Determine the distance the locomotive travels before its velocity is reduced to $12.0\text{ m/s}$.

A container ship unloads a cargo consignment with a registered weight of $4.25\text{ tons}$. Convert this mass measurement directly into grams ($\text{g}$), given that $1\text{ ton} = 1,000\text{ kg}$.
A crane lifts a heavy concrete slab vertically upward at a constant speed. Describe the nature of the work done by the gravitational force acting on the slab during this upward displacement.
questions.explanation: Gravity acts downward while displacement is upward. Force and displacement are in opposite directions, so work done by gravity is negative.

questions.question: A high-tensile steel spring in an industrial shock absorber is compressed by $0.140\text{ m}$ from its natural relaxed length. If the spring constant is $8.50 \times 10^4\text{ N/m}$, calculate the total elastic strain energy stored within the compressed spring.

A $0.220\text{ kg}$ projectile enters a dense wooden block at a speed of $240\text{ m/s}$. The block exerts an average resistive force of $6.20 \times 10^4\text{ N}$ to bring the projectile to a complete stop. Use the work-energy relationship to find the depth of penetration into the wood.

A $0.450\text{ kg}$ soccer ball rolls toward a player at $6.50\text{ m/s}$. The player kicks the ball directly backward in the opposite direction, giving it a velocity of $22.0\text{ m/s}$. If the impact lasts for $0.0180\text{ s}$, calculate the average force exerted on the ball by the player's foot.

An electric winch hoists a $740\text{ kg}$ pallet of bricks vertically through a height of $14.5\text{ m}$ in $22.0\text{ s}$. Assuming the pallet moves at a constant speed, determine the average power output delivered by the winch motor. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)
An electric pump consumes $4.80 \times 10^5\text{ J}$ of electrical energy and delivers $3.12 \times 10^5\text{ J}$ of useful mechanical work. Calculate the percentage efficiency of the pump system.

A stationary $6.80\text{ kg}$ explosive canister detonates on a frictionless test floor, splitting into two fragments. Fragment A ($2.10\text{ kg}$) flies due west at $45.0\text{ m/s}$. Determine the velocity of Fragment B ($4.70\text{ kg}$) immediately following the explosion.

A technician uses a wheel and axle system to lift an engine component. The wheel radius is $36.0\text{ cm}$ and the axle radius is $9.00\text{ cm}$. If the technician applies $140\text{ N}$ to lift a $480\text{ N}$ load, calculate the actual mechanical advantage (AMA).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on work-energy theorem, impulse-momentum, and unit conversions.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
